lexicon.title: Rhyme Lexicon & Synonyms
lexicon.no_results: Type a word to search for rhymes or synonyms.

studio.empty_lyrics: Your canvas lyrics will show up here as you write them.

canvas.wired_headphones_banner_text: Use wired headphones for a high-quality low-latency recording session

releasing_regret.title: Letting Go of Regret
releasing_regret.q0: What's one memory from your past that still feels heavy?
releasing_regret.q1: What would it feel like to finally let that go?
releasing_regret.q2: What are you holding onto that isn't helping you anymore?
releasing_regret.q3: Write one line about carrying something heavy.
releasing_regret.q4: What kind of music feels like relief?

finding_stillness.title: Finding Calm
finding_stillness.q0: What's making your mind feel busy right now?
finding_stillness.q1: What would it feel like if everything went quiet for a moment?
finding_stillness.q2: What does calm feel like to you?
finding_stillness.q3: Write one line about a quiet moment.
finding_stillness.q4: What kind of tempo feels calm — slow, steady, gentle?

growth_after_pain.title: Growing Stronger
growth_after_pain.q0: What hard time in your life changed you the most?
growth_after_pain.q1: What's one way you've grown since then?
growth_after_pain.q2: How did that hard time end up teaching you something?
growth_after_pain.q3: Write one line comparing your strength to something that grows back.
growth_after_pain.q4: What rhythm feels like slowly getting stronger?

embracing_uncertainty.title: Trusting the Unknown
embracing_uncertainty.q0: What's something in your life that feels uncertain right now?
embracing_uncertainty.q1: What would it feel like to take one step without knowing what's next?
embracing_uncertainty.q2: What helps you feel safe when things are unclear?
embracing_uncertainty.q3: Write one line about trusting yourself, even without answers.
embracing_uncertainty.q4: What kind of melody feels like stepping into the unknown?

grief_and_honoring.title: Honoring a Loss
grief_and_honoring.q0: What or who are you missing right now?
grief_and_honoring.q1: What's one good memory connected to this?
grief_and_honoring.q2: What would it feel like to let some of that sadness soften?
grief_and_honoring.q3: Write one line that honors what you lost.
grief_and_honoring.q4: What melody feels like healing to you?

overcoming_fear.title: Facing Fear
overcoming_fear.q0: What's something you're scared to do, but want to do anyway?
overcoming_fear.q1: What's the worst that could happen if you tried?
overcoming_fear.q2: What would it feel like on the other side of that fear?
overcoming_fear.q3: Write one line about turning fear into courage.
overcoming_fear.q4: What moment in a song feels like a big, brave leap?

self_compassion.title: Being Kind to Yourself
self_compassion.q0: Where have you been hard on yourself lately?
self_compassion.q1: What would you say to a friend going through the same thing?
self_compassion.q2: What does it feel like to finally give yourself a break?
self_compassion.q3: Write one gentle line just for you.
self_compassion.q4: What kind of sound feels comforting, like a warm hug?

reclaiming_voice.title: Speaking Your Truth
reclaiming_voice.q0: What have you been holding back from saying?
reclaiming_voice.q1: What would it feel like to finally say it out loud?
reclaiming_voice.q2: Who do you most want to hear this?
reclaiming_voice.q3: Write one line that says what you've been afraid to say.
reclaiming_voice.q4: What kind of sound feels loud and powerful?

patience_and_timing.title: Trusting Your Timing
patience_and_timing.q0: What are you rushing that maybe needs more time?
patience_and_timing.q1: What good things have come from waiting, in your life?
patience_and_timing.q2: What's helping you grow, even if you can't see it yet?
patience_and_timing.q3: Write one line about growing at your own pace.
patience_and_timing.q4: What slow rhythm feels patient to you?

strength_vulnerability.title: Strong Enough to Be Open
strength_vulnerability.q0: What's something real about you that you usually hide?
strength_vulnerability.q1: Why does showing that feel scary?
strength_vulnerability.q2: What could happen if you let people see the real you?
strength_vulnerability.q3: Write one honest line about being open, even when it's hard.
strength_vulnerability.q4: What instrument feels soft but strong?

navigating_darkness.title: Finding Light in Hard Times
navigating_darkness.q0: What's making things feel dark for you right now?
navigating_darkness.q1: What's one small good thing you can still see?
navigating_darkness.q2: What helps you keep going when things feel hard?
navigating_darkness.q3: Write one line about finding light in a hard moment.
navigating_darkness.q4: What sound feels hopeful, even when it's quiet?

cleansing_renewal.title: Starting Fresh
cleansing_renewal.q0: What do you want to leave behind?
cleansing_renewal.q1: What would it feel like to start clean, with nothing weighing you down?
cleansing_renewal.q2: Who would you be without other people's labels for you?
cleansing_renewal.q3: Write one line about starting over.
cleansing_renewal.q4: What beat feels fresh and new to you?

staying_grounded.title: Staying Grounded
staying_grounded.q0: What's making you feel scattered or overwhelmed right now?
staying_grounded.q1: What helps you feel steady when life feels like too much?
staying_grounded.q2: What's one thing you're grateful for in this moment?
staying_grounded.q3: Write one line about feeling safe and steady.
staying_grounded.q4: What low, steady sound feels grounding to you?

feeling_connected.title: Feeling Less Alone
feeling_connected.q0: When do you feel most alone?
feeling_connected.q1: Who do you wish understood what you're going through?
feeling_connected.q2: What does it feel like to know someone else has felt this too?
feeling_connected.q3: Write one line about reaching out to someone.
feeling_connected.q4: What harmony — two voices together — feels like connection to you?

accepting_change.title: Accepting Change
accepting_change.q0: What change in your life are you fighting right now?
accepting_change.q1: What might this change be teaching you?
accepting_change.q2: What would it feel like to go with it, instead of against it?
accepting_change.q3: Write one line about letting life change you.
accepting_change.q4: What kind of rhythm shift feels like change?

releasing_anger.title: Letting Out Anger
releasing_anger.q0: What's been making you angry or frustrated lately?
releasing_anger.q1: What's underneath the anger — hurt, fear, something else?
releasing_anger.q2: What would it feel like to finally let that anger out?
releasing_anger.q3: Write one strong line that says what you really feel.
releasing_anger.q4: What moment in a song feels like release — loud, then calm?

new_beginnings.title: New Beginnings
new_beginnings.q0: What's one thing you want to start fresh?
new_beginnings.q1: What would today look like with a clean start?
new_beginnings.q2: What's the first small step you could take?
new_beginnings.q3: Write one hopeful line about a new beginning.
new_beginnings.q4: What chord or note feels like the start of something good?

unconditional_worth.title: Knowing Your Worth
unconditional_worth.q0: Do you feel like you have to earn your worth? Why?
unconditional_worth.q1: What makes you valuable, even when you're not doing anything?
unconditional_worth.q2: What would it feel like to believe you're enough, just as you are?
unconditional_worth.q3: Write one line about your own worth.
unconditional_worth.q4: What kind of big, full sound feels like strength to you?

healing_child.title: Remembering How to Play
healing_child.q0: What did you love doing as a kid, before life got serious?
healing_child.q1: What would you make today if you weren't afraid of getting it wrong?
healing_child.q2: What would you want to tell your younger self?
healing_child.q3: Write one playful line that makes you smile.
healing_child.q4: What melody feels light and playful to you?

quiet_strength.title: Quiet Strength
quiet_strength.q0: What's a hard time you've stayed strong through, quietly?
quiet_strength.q1: How do you keep going when things get tough?
quiet_strength.q2: What does your strength look like when no one's watching?
quiet_strength.q3: Write one line about being strong in a quiet way.
quiet_strength.q4: What steady rhythm feels like quiet strength?

collab.invite_title: Invite someone to collaborate with you in this project
collab.confirm_close_title: Are you sure you want to close the collaboration?
collab.confirm_remove_collab_title: Are you sure you want to remove the collaborator(s)?
collab.remove_collab_desc: This will remove the collaborator(s) from this project. They will no longer have access to it.
collab.confirm_leave_collab_title: Are you sure you want to leave the collaboration?
collab.leave_collab_desc: This will remove you from this project. You will lose access to it.
collab.decline_desc: This will decline the invitation and clean the project from your canvas.
collab.close_desc: This will disconnect the project's real-time collaboration. If you are a collaborator, you will leave this project.

learn.access_restricted_desc: Please sign in to access your movements and continue your mastery journey.
learn.select_lesson: Select a lesson to view content
learn.video_not_supported: Your browser does not support the video tag.
learn.deep_dive_desc: More advanced music lessons are on their way. Check back soon.
learn.bank_of_ideas_desc: Curated ideas and shortcuts are on their way.
learn.bank_of_ideas_tooltip:
These tips are not rules.
They are simple ways to help you start, explore, and improve your lyrics.
Use what feels useful.
Skip what does not fit.
There is no single right way to write a song.
learn.no_lessons_yet: New lessons are on their way.
learn.placeholder_intro: Lesson notes for this chapter are coming soon. For now, use the video alongside these placeholder notes to get a feel for the new layout.
learn.placeholder_point_1: First key takeaway from this lesson.
learn.placeholder_point_2: Second key takeaway from this lesson.
learn.placeholder_point_3: Third key takeaway from this lesson.

connect.no_posts_found: No posts found in this category. Be the first to share!
connect.delete_post_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this post?
connect.delete_comment_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this comment?
connect.shared_new_lyric: Shared a new lyric constellation from workspace.

creative.or_start_inspiration: or just start with an inspiration
creative.inspiration_audio: an existing song, voice memo or beat
creative.inspiration_photo: a photo of your handwritten lyrics
creative.inspiration_doc: a PDF, text file or Word document of your lyrics

progress.title: Your progress
progress.subtitle: Complete all 3 goals to reach Level 2
progress.mind_power_desc: Songwriter Mind Power tracks the time you spend thinking and creating songs. Your mind is a muscle. The more you use it, the stronger it gets.
proverbs: Every line written brings you closer to your masterpiece.
Consistency is the key to unlocking your creative genius.
Great songs are not written, they are rewritten.
A single word can spark a whole symphony.
Small steps in songwriting lead to giant leaps in melody.
You are building your legacy, one lyric at a time.
Keep pouring your soul into the canvas; it is paying off.
Crafting art requires patience, and you are doing great.

profile.title: Profile & settings
profile.subtitle: Manage your account details and preferences.
profile.verify_sent: We've sent a verification link to
profile.verify_sent_end: . Please authorize the change in your email client to verify it.
profile.success_desc: Your email address has been updated to
profile.notifications_desc: Receive updates on new modules and masterclass events.
profile.public_profile_desc: Allow other students to see your constellation progress.
profile.error_update_email: Failed to update email. Please try logging in again.

welcome.subject: Welcome to Veinote, {{name}}
welcome.body_1: Welcome to Veinote. You've just opened a space built for one thing: finishing songs you're proud of.
welcome.body_2: Your canvas is ready whenever you are — lyrics, melody notes, and ideas all in one flow.

practice.select_theme_desc: Select the emotional anchor for your verses
practice.link_desc: Connect nouns with verbs 1 by 1 (order doesn't matter)
practice.localhost_warning: Make sure the audio stream is running on localhost.

studio_guide.title: Studio Guide
studio_guide.headphone_warning: For the best timing, use wired headphones. Bluetooth may cause recording delay.
studio_guide.env_tip_1: Find a quiet, carpeted room (avoid echo)
studio_guide.env_tip_2: Close windows & shut down background fans/AC
studio_guide.tech_tip_1: Position mic 10-15 cm from your mouth
studio_guide.tech_tip_2: Set level so peaks don't clip (avoid red zone)
studio_guide.equip_tip_2: Choose a high-quality USB/XLR condenser mic

studio_banner.warning_text: For the best timing, use wired headphones. Bluetooth may cause recording delay.
studio_banner.before_you_record_sub: You do not need professional equipment to use Demo Studio. Your device's built-in microphone is enough to get started.
studio_banner.tip_headphones_desc: Headphones prevent the previously recorded tracks and metronome from being recorded again through your microphone.
studio_banner.tip_wired_desc: Bluetooth headphones can create noticeable delay while recording. For better timing, use headphones connected with a cable.
studio_banner.tip_mic_desc: Before recording, make sure Demo Studio is using the microphone you want to record with.
studio_banner.tip_quiet_desc: Background noise and room echo will become part of your recording. A quiet room with curtains, furniture, or other soft surfaces usually sounds better.
studio_banner.tip_pos_desc: Stay close enough to get a clear sound, but not so close that the recording becomes distorted.
studio_banner.tip_apps_desc: Closing other audio, video, or browser applications can improve performance and reduce interruptions.
studio_banner.recommended_setup_sub: For a simple and reliable recording setup, we recommend:
studio_banner.higher_quality_note: For higher-quality recordings, you can use an audio interface with a microphone or instrument connected to it.
studio_banner.about_bluetooth_desc: Bluetooth headphones are useful for listening, but they are not recommended while recording. Bluetooth introduces latency, which means you may hear the sound slightly later than you play or sing.

support.title: Contact Support
support.message_placeholder: Write your message or complaint here...
support.success: Your message has been sent successfully! We will contact you back shortly.
support.error: Failed to send message. Please check your connection or try again.

feedback_modal.title: Your feedback makes Veinote better for everyone.
feedback_modal.message_placeholder: Write your feedback or ideas here...
feedback_modal.success: Your feedback has been sent. Thank you!
feedback_modal.error: Failed to send feedback. Please check your connection or try again.
feedback_modal.upload_placeholder: Attach an image, video, or PDF
feedback_modal.size_limit_error: File size exceeds the 3MB limit. Please select a smaller file.
